subject: Re: News regarding linux versions of WARP
body:
dear all, is there any update on a command-line version of WARP for Linux?

thanks!

Jesse

On Friday, June 9, 2023 at 12:42:19 PM UTC+2 alois.s...@gmail.com wrote:

> At the moment, I recommend using wine-staging 7.21 (compiling it with 
> cuda-support) - That's the one we use in production, that's what I 
> recommend at this time. 
> I tried to document everything at the appdb.winehq.org e.g. 
>
> https://appdb.winehq.org/objectManager.php?sClass=version&iId=40620&iTestingId=112856
> Later versions (7.22) of wine-staging will not work, because support for 
> cuda has been disabled. 
>
> You might want to try recent version of wine (compiled with "shared WoW64" 
> approach)  together with nvidia-libs. Currently, we see some potential 
> issue with dotnet/WPF, that prevents the use if this.  
>
>
> On Wednesday, April 26, 2023 at 6:57:17 PM UTC+2 xingyichen...@gmail.com 
> wrote:
>
>> Dear fellow WARP/M users and Dimitry,
>>
>> Does any of you have news regarding progress on Linux versions of WARP? 
>> Perhaps Dimitry or some of you already started testing with beta versions? 
>>
>> I read a few of you in the group managed to get WARP/Wine going, anyone 
>> got to run it more or less stably? If possible we'd greatly appreciate it 
>> if you could share more of your setup information! 
>>
>> Thank you so much!
>>
>> Cheers,
>>
>> Cyan Ching
>>
>> PhD student
>>
>> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>
>
